zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham於1509年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾馬普萊度哈姆時，他的父親Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire42歲，母親Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount21歲。他於從1528年1月11日到1529年1月10日與Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham在英國奧可福爾馬普萊度哈姆結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1564年8月11日在英國米德萊可倫敦享年55歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦堂爾 哈姆勒次。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham于1509年出生在英国英格兰奧可福爾馬普萊度哈姆时，他的父亲Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire42岁，母亲Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount21岁。他于从1528年1月11日到1529年1月10日与Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham在英国奧可福爾馬普萊度哈姆结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1564年8月11日在英国米德萊可倫敦享年55岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦堂爾 哈姆勒次。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: blount
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands): variant of   Blunt  .

variants.name: Blunt
meanings: English: nickname for someone with fair hair or a light complexion, from Old French blund, blond (from Latin blondus) ‘blond, fair, yellow-haired’, used also of complexion.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: iver
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: locative name from Iver (Bucks) named from Old English * ȳfre ‘edge or brow of a hill, tip of a promontory’, or from a minor place named with the same word.
2 Scottish: (i) relationship name, reduced form of Irish and Scottish Gaelic Mac Íomhair, see McIvor . (ii) relationship name from the Old Scandinavian personal name Ivar; see Ivor .

variants.name: Ivers
meanings: Irish: from Ó hÍomhair, ‘descendant of Íomhar’, a Gaelic form of the Old Norse personal name Ívarr (see Iverson 2), with excrescent English -s. It is occasionally Anglicized as Howard . Also a shortened form of Mac Íomhair ‘son of Íomhar’, with excrescent English -s. See McIver .
North German: patronymic from the personal name Ivar (see Iversen ).

English: variant of Iver, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a habitational name from Iver (Buckinghamshire) named from Old English ȳfre ‘edge or brow of a hill, tip of a promontory’, or from a minor place called with the same word.

variants.name: Ivor
meanings: English: (i) relationship name from Old Scandinavian Ívarr, Iwar, Ivar ‘yew-army’. (ii) relationship name, possibly from the Welsh male given name Ifor; if so, it is not clear that it survives today.

variants.name: Heaver
meanings: Americanized form of German   Hoefer  .
English: variant of   Haver  .

variants.name: Juer
meanings: Norman, English: perhaps a reduced form of   Jewers  . Some examples in the censuses may, however, be mistranscriptions of   Iver  .

variants.name: McIvor
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McIver  .

nameMeanings.name: richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham was born in 1509, in Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire, was 42 and his mother, Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount, was 21. He married Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham from 11 January 1528 to 10 January 1529, in Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 11 August 1564, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 55, and was buried in Tower Hamlets, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham est né en 1509, à Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham du 11 janvier 1528 au 10 janvier 1529, à Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 11 août 1564, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Tower Hamlets, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham 1509 in Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire 42 und seine Mutter Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount 21. Er hat von 11. Januar 1528 bis 10. Januar 1529 in Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, England Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 11. August 1564 im Alter von 55 Jahren in London, Middlesex, England und wurde in Tower Hamlets, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham è nato il nel 1509 nel Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham da 11 gennaio 1528 a 10 gennaio 1529, nel Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 11 agosto 1564, nel London, Middlesex, England, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Tower Hamlets, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurhamが1509年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアマプレドウルハムで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshireは42歳，母Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blountは21歳であった。から1528年1月11日まで1529年1月10日にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアマプレドウルハムでLady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1564年8月11日にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで55歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドントウエル ハムレツにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham(이)가 1509년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 마플레더람에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1528년 1월 11일부터 1529년 1월 10일까지 영국 스퍼드실르 마플레더람에서 Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1564년 8월 11일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 타워 함레츠에 묻혔다.

pt.1528: de 11 de janeiro de 1528 a 10 de janeiro de 1529
pt.Chapel Of Saint Peter Ad-Vincula, Tower: Tower Hamlets, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham nasceu em 1509, em Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham de 11 de janeiro de 1528 a 10 de janeiro de 1529, em Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de agosto de 1564, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Tower Hamlets, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ro.Chapel Of Saint Peter Ad-Vincula, Tower: Tower Hamlets, London, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham родился в 1509 г. в местности мапледурам, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham с 11 января 1528 по 10 января 1529 в местности мапледурам, оксфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 11 августа 1564 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности тоуир хамлец, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Chapel Of Saint Peter Ad-Vincula, Tower: Tower Hamlets, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir richard le blount of iver , 2nd baronet van mapledurham nació en 1509, en Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Richard Blount Ist. kg.kt , 4th baronet mountjoy, knight of the garter, knoight of bath, sheriff of buckinghamshire, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Lady Joane de Atmore Horne , baroness mountjoy, lady blount, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Lady elizabeth lyster , 2nd baroness van mapledurham desde 11 de enero de 1528 hasta 10 de enero de 1529, en Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 11 de agosto de 1564, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Tower Hamlets, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
